title: Blocks, CAPTCHAs, Rate Signals, Identity, and No-Evasion Boundary · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for blocks, captchas, rate signals, identity, and no-evasion boundary and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, network, DNS, parser, filesystem, privacy, accessibility, legal-review, scale, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for block detection and owner-escalation policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Detect denial, challenge, login, consent, interstitial, throttling, suspicious redirects, and semantic block pages from multiple signals.
Treat CAPTCHA and human-verification challenges as stop and owner-coordination signals rather than puzzles to automate around.
Use an honest stable identity and refuse proxy rotation, fingerprint spoofing, account cycling, stealth plugins, or other defense evasion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"
